recapPrompt.prompt: Which data structure helps BFS stay level by level?

choices.label: A queue
choices.feedback: Correct. A queue keeps nodes in discovered order, which matches level order.

choices.label: A stack
choices.feedback: Not this one. A stack pushes toward last-in-first-out behavior instead.

reflectionPrompt.prompt: Explain in your own words why a queue is useful for level-order traversal.

practiceChallenge.title: Mastery check: rebuild the tree clues
practiceChallenge.description: Complete the helper so it returns the main clue for each kind of tree job.
hints: The root is the top starting node.
BFS goes level by level with a queue.
BST search uses smaller-left and larger-right.
practiceChallenge.successMessage: Pass. You rebuilt the main tree clues from memory.
practiceChallenge.retryMessage: Needs practice. Recheck the root idea, the BFS clue, and the BST search rule.

blanks.label: Main clue for BST search
expectedValues: go left or right by value
